title: entities: change the VolumeInfo CreatedAt type to time.Time
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body: What's the intent here? I'm concerned this could be a breaking API change.

@mheon The intent is to make the CreatedAt attribute type similar to the other CreatedAt so that they can be easily compared. If I understand correctly, the json encoding converts this to a string and this shouldn't break the API (unless CreatedAt is not a date).

Looks like you need a go.fmt.

You also need to add a test.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *TristanCacqueray* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

The abandoning sounds correct - I did that as part of the volume driver work (we have reimplemented inspecting volumes, completely parallel implementations, in at least five places, which I replaced with Libpod's version). I didn't remove this because I suspected it was still in use, but if we still compile cleanly after, the struct can be torn out without concern.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@TristanCacqueray @mheon @What is the state of this PR?
Needs to be replaced with a new PR that removes this struct, at a minimum (it's currently unused) and possibly adjusts the types used by volume inspect.
